task_completion.description: Fix the blocking sleep call by replacing it with an asynchronous sleep.

task_completion.description: Fix the sequential execution of jobs by using concurrent asyncio patterns.

task_completion.visual_evidence: In the code editor, an 'asyncio.Lock()' is instantiated and used (e.g., 'async with lock:') around the code block that updates job status counters.

task_completion.description: Add the missing await keyword to the response.json() call.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not change the public API function signatures.

integrity.description: Ensure the test results are from genuinely running pytest or the Python test runner.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal output shows actual pytest/unittest collection and execution logs, not just a mocked 'echo' command or manually typed success message.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/PycharmProjects/async_scheduler. There are exactly 4 bugs injected into the server/ directory that the agent must fix: 1) A synchronous 'time.sleep()' call that must be changed to 'await asyncio.sleep()'. 2) A sequential for-loop with 'await' that must be changed to run concurrently (e.g., using asyncio.gather). 3) A missing asyncio.Lock around shared mutable state (status counters). 4) A missing 'await' on a 'response.json()' call. The agent must pass all tests without modifying test files or public API signatures.
